title: review pass: lib.rs headers of all crates
description: include links to project home and online docs

title: workflow: replace github action versions with sha
basil-nats: make crate no_std + alloc compatible
description: Investigation (2026-07-05) found basil-nats is a pure protocol/crypto crate (NATS ed25519-nkey JWTs, NKey encode/decode, xkey seal/verify) with zero async/tokio/socket/file/thread/env usage — all NATS transport already lives in the separate basil-nats-bridge crate. Workspace crypto deps (ed25519-dalek, x25519-dalek, sha2, zeroize) are already pinned default-features=false / alloc-only, so no_std+alloc is reachable with only two localized fixes (tracked as child issues): the std::collections::BTreeMap import and the thread_rng() call in seal_nats_curve. No existing no_std attempt or stated design goal in CHANGELOG/docs — this is net-new scope, not documented intent. Umbrella issue: add #![no_std] + extern crate alloc to src/lib.rs, add std/alloc cargo features gating optional std-only conveniences, and verify a no_std+alloc build once child issues land.
close_reason: basil-nats crate source is now no_std + alloc: added no_std attribute, extern crate alloc, core::fmt/core::error, and std (default) / alloc cargo features; verified cargo build -p basil-nats --no-default-features --features alloc (commit ad92b9b)

title: basil-nats: replace std::collections::BTreeMap with alloc equivalent
description: src/lib.rs:37 imports std::collections::BTreeMap. Swap to alloc::collections::BTreeMap (requires extern crate alloc once #![no_std] lands in the parent issue). Trivial, mechanical change — no behavior difference.

title: basil-nats: remove thread_rng() dependency from seal_nats_curve
description: src/lib.rs:354, inside seal_nats_curve, calls rand::thread_rng().fill_bytes(&mut nonce) — the one genuine std/getrandom blocker in the crate (everything else, including time validation via a caller-supplied now_unix: u64, is already OS-independent). Fix: change seal_nats_curve to accept an impl RngCore parameter (the RngCore trait is already imported/used in this file) instead of calling thread_rng() internally, and update call sites to pass a rng (e.g. thread_rng() at call sites that still have std, or a caller-chosen RNG under no_std).
close_reason: seal_nats_curve now takes an explicit rng: &mut impl RngCore (rand_core); internal thread_rng removed, all call sites updated to pass rand::thread_rng under std (commit ad92b9b)

title: basil config init ignores socket parameter
description: when invoking "basil config init" the socket written to basil-agent.toml is always <config.dir>/basil.sock, even if --socket parameter is passed  with a different path,  or if BASIL_SOCKET is set in the environment
close_reason: init now honors socket precedence explicit --socket gt BASIL_SOCKET gt dir/basil.sock; 3-case unit test added (commit 11187755)

title: Flatten CLI: remove the `basil config` namespace
description:
Collapse the `basil config *` subcommands into first-tier verbs so the CLI is flat and verb-first. End state: the `config` namespace is gone entirely.

Moves (see child issues):
- `basil config check`   -> folded into `basil doctor`
- `basil config init`    -> `basil init`
- `basil config explain` -> `basil explain`

That leaves `config` with zero children, so the `Config` subcommand and `ConfigCommand` enum (basil-bin/src/lib.rs) get deleted.

Cross-cutting decisions:
- NO deprecation shims/aliases. Delete the old paths outright. Repo went public 2026-07-04 and the CLI is unannounced -> no external users to break.
- All syntax changes documented in CHANGELOG.
- A follow-up issue sweeps tests, demo scripts, and downstream repos for the renamed commands.

Current surface for reference (basil-bin/src/lib.rs): top-level `agent`, `bundle`, `config {init,check,explain}`, `doctor`, flattened client verbs; `--socket` is global.
close_reason: CLI flattened: the basil config namespace is gone. config check -> doctor (--keys/--strict, fatal-vs-warn exit model); config init -> basil init (honors --socket/BASIL_SOCKET); config explain -> basil explain (offline default, --live for the running broker, over-socket explain folded in). All child moves (basil-1zx.1/.2/.3) + sweep (.4) closed; no deprecation shims (unannounced CLI). Docs + CHANGELOG updated.

title: Fold `basil config check` into `basil doctor`; delete config check
description:
Merge `config check` behavior into `doctor` and remove `basil config check` (run_check / CheckArgs in agent_cli.rs; Check variant in ConfigCommand).

What config check does today that doctor does not, and must move into doctor's OFFLINE (no-unlock) tier:
- offline capability enforcement (enforce_capabilities, honors capability-policy)
- invocation broker-identity/key binding validation (validate_invocation_catalog_bindings)
- per-key present/missing detail (doctor currently collapses this to one key_material row)

Tiers (boundary = does it unlock the sealed bundle and authenticate?):
- `basil doctor`         offline, no unlock: catalog/policy load, capability, invocation bindings, backend binary on PATH, socket, bundle perms/freshness, backend health reachability.
- `basil doctor --keys`  unlock bundle + authenticated per-key existence probe (today's `--check-keys` + config check's per-key detail). Rename `--check-keys` -> `--keys`.

EXIT-STATUS MODEL (straw-man):
- Exit non-zero ONLY for FATAL errors: conditions that would stop the broker/service from starting.
- Everything else is a WARNING. With no fatal errors, exit 0 (report-only).
- `--strict`: warnings also exit non-zero. There is NO `--require`; `--strict` replaces it.
- Return code derived from the set of checks that ran and their worst severity.

Per-check severity needs deciding, e.g.: backend unreachable / bundle won't unlock -> FATAL; a missing=error key that reconcile cannot satisfy -> FATAL; missing=generate key, optional key absent, `bao` not on PATH -> WARNING. (Today key_material_check returns a *blocking* fail on any missing=error key and run_doctor exits 1 on any blocking failure; this model refines that into fatal-vs-warn.)

Acceptance:
- `basil config check` no longer exists.
- `basil doctor` / `--keys` / `--strict` cover every check config check performed.
- Exit codes follow the model above.
close_reason: Folded config check into doctor: offline capability + invocation-binding checks, per-key detail under doctor --keys, --check-keys renamed --keys, --require replaced by --strict, fatal-vs-warning exit model, DOCTOR_SCHEMA_VERSION bumped to 2 (commit a38dd95b)

title: Move `basil config init` to first-tier `basil init`; delete config init
description:
Promote `config init` to `basil init` (idiomatic: git/cargo/terraform init). Delete the `config init` path.

Growth path (design notes, not all required in v1):
- Expand from a single-file scaffold to a templated PROJECT: config TOML + catalog.json + policy.json, plus a secret-safe `.gitignore` (must ignore *.sealed, token files, passphrase files -- the from-scratch fixture writes tokens to /tmp precisely to keep them out of the tree).
- Consider `basil init --template <name>` (e.g. openbao-dev, vault-prod, keystore) instead of a growing flag pile; a template picks defaults for the existing --unlock/--backend flags.

Related: basil-u00 (config init ignores --socket / BASIL_SOCKET) touches this same code -- fix or fold in during the move.

Acceptance:
- `basil init` works; `basil config init` no longer exists.
close_reason: Promoted config init to first-tier basil init; ConfigCommand::Init removed, config explain kept (commit 11187755)

title: Move `basil config explain` to first-tier `basil explain`; delete config explain
description:
Promote `config explain` to `basil explain`. It is a distinct verb (offline policy dry-run: evaluate a subject/action/target through the real PDP -- no secrets, no backend, no socket) and should NOT live under `doctor` (health/preflight) -- different question.

Deletes the `config explain` path. Combined with the check and init moves this empties the `config` namespace, so remove the `Config` subcommand + `ConfigCommand` enum (basil-bin/src/lib.rs).

Acceptance:
- `basil explain ...` works with the current ExplainArgs surface; `basil config explain` no longer exists.

title: Sweep tests, scripts, and downstream repos for renamed CLI verbs; update CHANGELOG
description:
Follow-up once the config-namespace moves land. Update every reference from old `basil config *` syntax to the new verbs.

- CHANGELOG: document all syntax changes (config check -> doctor [--keys/--strict]; config init -> init; config explain -> explain; --check-keys -> --keys; --require -> --strict).
- In-repo: integration/e2e tests, demo/from-scratch, demo/demo.sh, demo/screen-recording-script.md, README/quickstart, completions + man-page generation (basil-bin cli()).
- Downstream repos: basil-go, edge / edgebox-rs, basil-doc -- grep for `config check`, `config init`, `config explain`, `--check-keys`, `--require`.

Blocked by the three move issues.
close_reason: Swept in-repo tests/scripts/nix/proto-comments to the flat verbs; consolidated CHANGELOG (commit 94a341f6). ../basil-doc fully updated + verified against the binary and committed there (a04c59ff). Downstream: ../edge and ../edgebox-rs assessed not-stale (historical/false-positive); the only genuine remaining staleness is Go-client doc-comments, split to follow-up basil-elw.

comments.author: user
comments.text:
In-repo sweep DONE (code committed 94a341f6). Edits:
- crates/basil-core/src/core/reconcile.rs: --require -> --strict (6 doc/test comment refs to the CI gate flag)
- crates/basil-core/src/agent_cli.rs: 2 comments 'offline basil config ... commands' -> 'basil doctor/explain'
- crates/basil-tests/tests/init_flow_e2e.rs: stale 'check --require' comment -> 'doctor --keys' (matches actual invocation)
- crates/basil-proto/.../broker.proto: 'basil config explain --json' -> 'basil explain --json' (basil-xax)
- nix/tests/tpm-unlock-test.nix + scripts/tpm-unlock-e2e.sh: 'config check' -> 'doctor' (5 each; real invocations + comments)
- CHANGELOG.md: consolidated the CLI-flattening bullets into a coherent non-redundant set (namespace removed; check->doctor +--keys/--strict + fatal-vs-warning exit; init->init honors --socket/BASIL_SOCKET; explain->explain offline default +--live, over-socket explain removed). Fixed the wrong 'config explain is unchanged' note.
False-positives left: client_cli.rs config_namespace_is_gone test (asserts old verb is GONE); bundle_cli.rs 'old basil config bundle' (deliberate historical ref).
Completions/man: NOT checked in (just man-pages -> target/man; no committed completions) - no action.
DOWNSTREAM still to sweep (read-only hit counts): ../basil-doc ~59 hits/16 files; ../edge ~11/5; ../basil-go 3/3; ../edgebox-rs 1/1; clients/go submodule 3 (broker.proto/broker.pb.go/admin.go basil-xax twins).
Gates: clippy --workspace --all-features + just test-rust PASS. Leaving OPEN for the downstream pass.

title: add aarch64 to arch linux github workflow

title: add Go tests to workflow
description: requires checking out submodule and running test-go, test-stream-interop
close_reason: ci.yml: added go-tests job running 'just test-go' + 'just test-stream-interop' over the clients/go submodule (commit 5903fa39)

title: basil-nats: make full dependency graph bare-metal no_std (workspace-wide default-features=false)
description: basil-nats source is now #![no_std]+alloc and compiles under --no-default-features --features alloc, but serde, serde_json, base64, and data-encoding are inherited with their default (std) feature sets, so the linked graph still pulls std on a host target. Cargo forbids overriding default-features at the member level when the workspace dep did not set it (hard error: default-features = false cannot override workspace default-features). Making the graph truly bare-metal needs those four deps declared default-features=false at workspace.dependencies with an alloc feature, and every std consumer (basil-core, basil-bin, etc.) re-adding the dep std feature. Broad blast radius; deferred out of the no_std enablement task.
close_reason: Fixed: workspace serde/serde_json/base64/data-encoding now default to alloc/no-defaults, basil-nats std forwards dependency std features, and std consumers opt back into std. Also removed rand_core/crypto_secretbox defaults from the alloc-only basil-nats graph. Verified alloc-only and std builds.

title: typo in clients/go/README.md blocks just check (typos step)
description: just check fails at the typos step on clients/go/README.md:5 (Documentationn -> Documentation). Pre-existing, in the clients/go git submodule (commit 554bd67), unrelated to Rust changes. All cargo build/clippy/test/rustfmt steps pass. Fix requires a commit inside the clients/go submodule, or a typos allowlist entry.

title: Reconcile two 'explain' verbs before promoting offline explain to 'basil explain' (blocks basil-1zx.3 / basil-1zx)
description:
basil-1zx.3 asks to promote offline "basil config explain" to top-level "basil explain", but that name is ALREADY taken by an existing over-socket command: client_cli::Command::Explain (flattened into Command::Client in crates/basil-bin/src/lib.rs), impl at crates/basil-bin/src/client_cli.rs L452 (variant) / L1311 (async fn explain). clap panics at runtime with "command name explain is duplicated" if a second top-level Explain is added.

Two distinct verbs, both subject/op/key -> allow/deny through the real PDP:
- OVER-SOCKET (existing "basil explain"): queries the RUNNING broker's serving generation; needs socket + dedicated "explain" admin permission; args subject/op/key/json only.
- OFFLINE (agent_cli ExplainArgs / run_explain, currently behind "config explain"): builds PDP locally from catalog+policy FILES; no socket/backend; richer surface: adds effective plus ConfigOverrides (config/catalog/policy/bundle/socket/vault-addr).

The issue author appears unaware the over-socket explain exists (parent basil-1zx lists the surface as "flattened client verbs" generically and describes explain as offline/no-socket). Promoting offline explain to the name "explain" is therefore blocked pending a design decision:
  (a) UNIFY into one "basil explain" verb with a mode selector (e.g. an offline flag plus the ConfigOverrides/effective args route to the offline agent_cli impl; default = over-socket live query). Cross-crate (basil-bin client dispatch -> basil-core offline impl), changes the explain arg surface, and updates client_cli explain parse tests + basil-1zx.4 sweep.
  (b) Rename one of them (e.g. keep offline as "basil explain", move the live one to "basil query"/"basil check-access") -- product/naming decision.
  (c) Drop one as redundant (NOT recommended; they serve different phases: pre-deploy offline vs operational live).

Consequence: basil-1zx ("empty the config namespace") cannot fully land via basil-1zx.3 until this is decided, because offline explain is the last config subcommand and has no collision-free home matching the issue's intended name. basil-1zx.1 (check->doctor) and basil-1zx.2 (init->basil init) are unaffected.

No code landed for basil-1zx.3; working tree reverted to green. Recommend orchestrator/human pick (a)/(b)/(c) and rescope basil-1zx.3.
close_reason: resolved: unified into one basil explain (offline default, --live for broker) (commit 714adfa8)

title: Stale CLI name in broker.proto comment: basil config explain --json
description: crates/basil-proto/proto/basil/broker/v1/broker.proto:923 documents the live AgentExplanation message as mirroring 'basil config explain --json'. After basil-1zx.3 unified the explain verbs, the offline path is 'basil explain --json' (the 'config' namespace is gone). Update the comment to reference 'basil explain --json'. Comment-only; no wire/regeneration impact. Out of scope for the basil-1zx.3 code commit (path-scoped to basil-bin/basil-core); fits the CLI-rename doc sweep tracked by basil-1zx.4.
close_reason: Fixed stale 'basil config explain --json' -> 'basil explain --json' in crates/basil-proto/proto/basil/broker/v1/broker.proto (committed 94a341f6). clients/go submodule twins deferred to downstream pass under basil-1zx.4.

title: Go client: sweep stale 'basil config explain' doc-comments to 'basil explain'
description: Downstream tail of basil-1zx.4. Genuine staleness in the Go client (same content in ../basil-go and the clients/go submodule): admin.go (~L216) and proto/basil/broker/v1/broker.proto (~L919) doc-comments reference 'basil config explain --json' which should be 'basil explain --json'. The generated internal/pb/broker.pb.go carries the proto comment (regenerate after). ../edge and ../edgebox-rs assessed NOT stale (historical changelog re config bundle + false positives).
